scope: diff vs pinned SHA on branch eval-uncommitted

findings.title: Race condition -- connection lock replaced with nullcontext by default
findings.description: _skip_connection_lock looked up with getattr(..., True) -- default True; every BrowserSession instance not explicitly setting attribute uses nullcontext() instead of self._connection_lock. Two concurrent callers race into connect().

findings.title: Off-by-one in history window -- items silently dropped
findings.description: Two coordinated errors: <= -> < at boundary; recent_items_count -1 -> -2. LLM sees fewer history items than configured; context silently lost.

findings.title: Silent error swallow -- non-retryable ModelProviderErrors discarded
findings.description: Restructured handler wraps retry-decision in try/except Exception: pass. Non-retryable or exhausted-retry ModelProviderError no longer raises. Caller never receives an exception.

findings.title: Hardcoded API key default committed to source
findings.description: DEFAULT_OPENAI_API_KEY = 'sk-eval-...' baked into source. os.environ.get('OPENAI_API_KEY', DEFAULT_OPENAI_API_KEY) without env var passes literal downstream. Secret scanners flag it.

findings.title: Command injection via os.system with unsanitized URL
findings.description: os.system(f'start {url}') / os.system(f'xdg-open {url}') passes url directly to shell without quoting. URL like 'https://x.com && rm -rf ~' executes arbitrary commands.

findings.title: Path traversal in read_named_file -- arbitrary host file read
findings.description: path = self.base_dir / name then open(path, 'r') with no further validation. name like '../../etc/passwd' resolves outside base_dir. No commonpath/resolve guard.

findings.title: Resource leak -- file handle not closed on early return
findings.description: Refactored code opens pyproject_path with bare open() (no with, no try/finally). On return version path the file handle f is never closed.

findings.title: Weak PRNG for device identity -- predictable device IDs
findings.description: device_id generated with random.choices over hex alphabet of length 32. MT19937 not a CSPRNG; ~624 outputs reconstruct internal state. Used in cloud sync OAuth2 Device Authorization Grant.

findings.title: Missing null guard -- AttributeError/TypeError when sensitive_data is None
findings.description: Early-exit guard removed. sensitive_data is None raises AttributeError on .items(). Falsy values now participate in equality comparisons, causing incorrect sensitive-key attribution.

findings.title: Timing side-channel in token comparison
findings.description: _check_token uses provided == expected; short-circuits on first mismatching byte. Remote attacker on sync webhook/auth endpoint can recover token byte-by-byte. Outer is_authorized_request docstring promises 'constant-time check'.

findings.title: eval whitelist bypassable via str subclass with overridden __eq__
findings.description: Python 'in' operator for tuple calls __eq__ on the token (LHS), not on whitelist strings. A str subclass overriding __eq__ to always return True passes both isinstance and the membership check; eval(token) then receives arbitrary Python.
findings.raw: if token not in _ALLOWED_LITERALS: raise; return eval(token)
